description:
**Problem**
REALTIME_PLAN §6.1/§6.2 calls for a store‑local `index_durability_mode = cache | durable` setting. The code always opens `SqliteWalIndex` with `IndexDurabilityMode::Cache` (e.g., `StoreRuntime::open`, fsck/rebuild helpers). There is no config knob or store‑local setting.

**Design**
- Add a store‑local setting (namespaces.toml or a new `store_config.toml`) or config‑layer override to choose `cache` vs `durable`.
- Thread the setting into `SqliteWalIndex::open` calls, including rebuild/fsck paths.

**Acceptance**
- [ ] Index durability mode is configurable and persisted per store.
- [ ] All index open paths respect the configured mode.
- [ ] Tests cover both modes.

**Files:** src/daemon/store_runtime.rs, src/daemon/wal/index.rs, src/daemon/wal/fsck.rs, src/config.rs (or new config module)
title: WalIndex durability mode is hardcoded to cache

acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] `RecordHeader` cannot represent “request_sha256 without client_request_id”.
- [ ] Encoding/decoding logic uses the new sum type and no longer needs runtime combo checks.
- [ ] All callers updated; tests added for each `RequestProof` variant.
description:
**Problem**
`RecordHeader` allows invalid combinations (e.g., `request_sha256` present without `client_request_id`), and this is enforced by runtime checks in encode/decode. That means invalid headers are representable in the type system, violating the “types tell the truth” principle.

Key references:
- `crates/beads-rs/src/daemon/wal/record.rs:65` — `RecordHeader` fields allow invalid combinations.
- `crates/beads-rs/src/daemon/wal/record.rs:103` — runtime validation in `encode`.

Severity: integrity checks are scattered across encode/decode paths; misuse can create invalid headers before validation.
design:
**Design**
Introduce a dedicated request‑proof sum type and make it the only representation.

- `enum RequestProof { None, Client { client_request_id: ClientRequestId, request_sha256: [u8;32] }, ClientNoHash { client_request_id: ClientRequestId } }`.
- `RecordHeader` stores a single `request_proof: RequestProof` rather than two optional fields.
- Encoding/decoding handles the different cases explicitly; invalid combinations become unrepresentable.

Migration:
- Add conversion helpers to maintain wire format compatibility.
- Update all call sites constructing `RecordHeader`.
title: Encode WAL request proof as sum type in RecordHeader

acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Legacy deps checkpoint payload is classified as incompatible (not generic JSON error).
- [ ] Daemon boot continues and schedules checkpoint rebuild when incompatible checkpoint is seen.
- [ ] Restore watermark behavior is correct when checkpoint is rejected.
- [ ] Tests cover classification and rebuild scheduling path.
description:
**Problem**
Checkpoint import currently treats legacy deps shape failures as generic JSON decode errors and silently skips restore, without explicit incompatible classification or rebuild trigger.

**Files**: crates/beads-git/src/checkpoint/import.rs, crates/beads-daemon/src/runtime/core/checkpoint_import.rs, crates/beads-daemon/src/runtime/core/repo_load.rs
design:
**Design**
Introduce typed incompatible-checkpoint classification for legacy deps payloads:
- add explicit checkpoint import error variant/helper for incompatible deps format
- plumb classification through daemon load path
- on incompatible classification: continue boot and force/schedule rebuild from canonical store

Keep checkpoint import strict; do not add legacy checkpoint parsing.

notes.author: darin@book
notes.content: Audit on 2026-03-10 against /Users/darin/src/personal/beads-rs-legacy-deps-v1-cutover: typed incompatible checkpoint classification landed, but explicit rebuild scheduling / coverage still looks incomplete.

notes.author: darin@book
notes.content: Audit on 2026-03-11 against /Users/darin/src/personal/beads-rs-legacy-deps-v1-cutover @ f0de8dc3: incompatible legacy checkpoint payloads are now classified distinctly, skipped without applying checkpoint state or watermarks, and trigger exact-group rebuild scheduling via force_checkpoint_group. Generic checkpoint decode failures remain non-migration skips. Added daemon coverage for incompatible-cache rebuild scheduling, watermark suppression, duplicate handling, and non-legacy regression behavior. Workspace gates are green (`cargo test`, `just dylint`, `cargo clippy --all-features -- -D warnings`).

title: Migration slice 6: Classify incompatible checkpoints and trigger rebuild
